props.question: Do Washington and Tehran both publicly confirm a signed memorandum by 16 June?

agents.name: Sprockett
agents.why: Both sides say the text is agreed and signing is days away; the momentum is real and public.

agents.name: Foreman
agents.why: Prices a base case of signature, with the energy complex already trading the relief.

agents.name: Graves
agents.why: Marginal yes; the physical chain says the desk shouldn't pay up for it.

agents.name: Cogsworth
agents.why: A coin flip on the calendar, not the substance.

agents.name: Tinkerton
agents.why: Announced US–Iran frameworks rarely close on the announced day; the hardest items are deferred 60 days and the FM says the text may still shift. Counter on signature by 16 Jun: 0.38.

no.note: The dissenter is the designated one. Read the gap, not the consensus.

items.note: Agreed text, unsigned: a 14-point memorandum negotiated over warning shots and a navy moving 7M bbl/d under guard.
Outside the text. Israel won't withdraw; Iran wants Lebanon in. The cleanest channel for a signature to slip.
China reports inspecting 198 vessels asserting jurisdiction; Taiwan vows expulsions. Inspection as coercion.
UNAMA documents 13 civilian deaths from cross-border airstrikes; competing casualty claims, no de-escalation step yet.
A 6×6m Chinese platform appears and vanishes; Manila protests; Beijing sanctions the Philippine defense chief.
Houthis announce a ban on Israeli shipping; war-risk and rerouting return as a conditional second front.

items.what: EU agrees to open accession talks with Ukraine and Moldova on 15 June in Luxembourg — enlargement as deterrence by another name.
NATO's top commander says the alliance must replan European defence around announced US cuts to forces, aircraft, and warships.
EU proposes a 21st Russia sanctions package targeting close to 90 banks, crypto rails, and oil traders; passage needs unanimity.
UK commits to a full import ban on Russian diesel and jet fuel by 1 January 2027, reviewed fortnightly.
Crimea hits its worst fuel shortage since 2014 after Ukrainian strikes; rationing at 20 litres per vehicle per week.
DPRK–Russia land bridge reported due for completion 19 June; analysts flag sanctions-circumvention potential.
Houthis threaten Israeli shipping in the Red Sea; a maritime-risk firm urges affiliation screening.
China sanctions the Philippine defense secretary over 'erroneous remarks' as the Scarborough platform dispute escalates.
US and South Korea hold nuclear-deterrence talks; analysts estimate a new DPRK enrichment hall could add 75% capacity.
UNAMA calls for de-escalation after confirming 13 civilian deaths in Pakistani cross-border airstrikes.
Olivia Rodrigo releases her third album to Pitchfork 'Best New Music' and a projected Billboard No. 1 — the week's other half-billion-stream event.
The DR Congo squad's leopard-print arrival becomes the World Cup's first viral image before a ball is kicked in their group.
